note: The correct half of the name-sorting pair. Sorting on the case-folded form compares letters rather than character codes, while the list still holds the names as they were typed. Every claim below was verified by running it.

labels.text: names are ordered alphabetically regardless of how they are capitalised
labels.why: verified: sort_names(['apple', 'Banana', 'cherry']) returns ['apple', 'Banana', 'cherry']. The key folds case for the comparison only.

labels.text: the returned list contains exactly the names that were given
labels.why: verified: the key affects ordering, not the values, so the names come back exactly as they were typed.
